mechanism_narrative: A falling or flat 3-month change in FEDFUNDS lowers discount-rate pressure on equity cash flows and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when evaluated as an always-on policy-rate momentum signal.
failure_condition: Reject if months with non-increasing 3-month FEDFUNDS changes do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with rising FEDFUNDS, or if the walk-forward hit rate is below 50% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below a recession-sensitive threshold signals weakening residential investment and future cyclical demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST-below-1100 episodes have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, expected policy tightening and margin pressure rise faster than nominal growth support, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
regime_hint: inflation-pressure regime with constrained policy rate
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS interaction has non-negative average 20-day forward returns after transaction-date alignment, or if an additive CPI-only and FEDFUNDS-only model matches the interaction signal out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous PAYEMS expansion and a falling UNRATE indicate broad labor-income growth without rising slack, supporting household demand and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive while UNRATE 3-month change crosses below 0 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-falling signal fails to outperform either single labor proxy over rolling 3-year walk-forward tests, or if triggered 20-day returns are positive in less than 52% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals improving production volumes and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations adjust.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum does not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO momentum in at least two-thirds of rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income prospects, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-above-5.5% crossing events are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if drawdown frequency is not above the unconditional frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in RSAFS signals resilient consumer spending and near-term revenue strength for listed companies, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days after the release.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month growth crossings above zero have a 5-day S&P 500 mean return less than or equal to the unconditional 5-day mean across walk-forward release-date tests.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarterly growth print in GDPC1 signals broad real-demand contraction and lower aggregate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses below 0%
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 contractions are not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in out-of-sample vintages, or if the sign accuracy of the negative signal is below 50% over two consecutive business cycles.
